pack_sources.author: AI-assisted implementation draft (unreviewed)

misconceptions.description: Multiplies exponents when multiplying powers with the same base instead of adding them.
misconceptions.remediation_hint: Keep the common base and add exponents; multiplication of exponents belongs to a power raised to another power.

misconceptions.description: Subtracts exponents in a product as though the operation were division.
misconceptions.error_signature: For x^a*x^b, produces x^(a-b) or x^(b-a).
misconceptions.remediation_hint: Name the outer operation first: multiplication of equal bases calls for adding exponents.

misconceptions.description: Treats a negative exponent as a negative coefficient rather than a reciprocal power.
misconceptions.remediation_hint: Move the powered factor across the fraction bar; the expression's sign does not change.

metaphors.description: An operation sorter: first identify product, quotient, or nested power, then send the exponents to the matching add, subtract, or multiply lane while the base stays labeled.

error_patterns: Uses exponent multiplication for a same-base product.
Uses exponent subtraction for a same-base product.
Changes the sign of a value when rewriting a negative exponent.
sources: OpenStax Algebra and Trigonometry 2e, Section 1.2: Exponents and Scientific Notation.
Common Core State Standards for Mathematics, HSN-RN.A: Extend the properties of exponents to rational exponents.

pack_sources.author: AI-assisted implementation draft (unreviewed)

misconceptions.description: Reduces the exponent but omits the original exponent as a multiplier.
misconceptions.remediation_hint: Write the original exponent in front before reducing the remaining exponent by one.

misconceptions.description: Uses the exponent as a multiplier but leaves the exponent on the variable unchanged.
misconceptions.remediation_hint: After bringing the exponent down, move the remaining exponent down one step.

misconceptions.description: Increases a negative exponent toward zero instead of subtracting one.
misconceptions.remediation_hint: Subtracting one makes a negative exponent one unit more negative.

metaphors.description: The exponent leaves one copy in front as the multiplier, while the exponent still attached to x moves down exactly one step.

error_patterns: Changes the exponent without creating a coefficient.
Creates the coefficient but does not reduce the exponent.
Moves a negative exponent toward zero after differentiation.
sources: OpenStax Calculus Volume 1, Section 3.3: Differentiation Rules.
College Board AP Calculus AB and BC Course and Exam Description, Unit 2: Differentiation.

pack_sources.author: AI-assisted implementation draft (unreviewed)

misconceptions.description: Differentiates only the first term of a sum and copies the remaining terms unchanged.
misconceptions.error_signature: The response contains the derivative of the first term followed by unchanged original terms.
misconceptions.remediation_hint: Give every additive term its own derivative pass before recombining the results.

misconceptions.description: Changes a subtraction sign into addition while differentiating term by term.
misconceptions.error_signature: A derivative term that should retain a leading minus sign appears positive.
misconceptions.remediation_hint: Carry each term's existing sign with it through differentiation.

misconceptions.description: Copies an additive constant into the derivative instead of replacing it with zero.
misconceptions.error_signature: The original nonzero constant remains as an additive term in the derivative.
misconceptions.remediation_hint: A fixed number has no change, so its derivative contributes zero.

metaphors.description: Each signed term travels independently through the differentiation station, then the transformed terms rejoin in their original order.

error_patterns: Leaves one or more nonconstant terms undifferentiated.
Drops or reverses a subtraction sign between derivative terms.
Retains an additive constant in the derivative.
sources: OpenStax Calculus Volume 1, Section 3.3: Differentiation Rules.
College Board AP Calculus AB and BC Course and Exam Description, Unit 2: Differentiation.

pack_sources.author: AI-assisted implementation draft (unreviewed)

misconceptions.description: Differentiates a product by multiplying the two derivatives and omits both hold-one-factor terms.
misconceptions.error_signature: For u*v, produces u'*v' instead of u'*v+u*v'.
misconceptions.remediation_hint: Differentiate one factor at a time while holding the other unchanged, then add the two terms.

misconceptions.description: Reverses the two terms in the quotient-rule numerator, negating an otherwise correct derivative.
misconceptions.error_signature: For u/v, uses u*v'-v*u' in the numerator.
misconceptions.remediation_hint: Start with denominator times numerator derivative, then subtract numerator times denominator derivative.

misconceptions.description: Uses the original denominator rather than its square in the quotient-rule result.
misconceptions.error_signature: The quotient-rule numerator is plausible, but the denominator is v rather than v^2.
misconceptions.remediation_hint: After forming the numerator, square the entire original denominator.

metaphors.description: For a product, each factor takes one turn changing while its partner waits; for a quotient, the denominator leads the ordered numerator and then supports the result twice.

error_patterns: Multiplies factor derivatives instead of adding two product-rule terms.
Reverses the quotient-rule numerator and returns the negative result.
Fails to square the full denominator in a quotient derivative.
sources: OpenStax Calculus Volume 1, Section 3.3: Differentiation Rules.
College Board AP Calculus AB and BC Course and Exam Description, Unit 2: Differentiation.
